content.problemStatement:
John Nash got another secret task from William Parcher and he needs your help. John found two strings SSS and TTT in the newspaper. Later, he found an integer NNN (as the registration number of his car). He needs to find out whether string TTT is a subsequence of the string SS...SSS...SSS...S (NNN times) - string that you get when you concatenate string SSS with itself NNN times.

Complete the function "patternNash" in the code snippet that takes three arguments: strings SSS and TTT and an integer NNN. The function should return a string "YES" or "NO" - depending on whether the string TTT is subsequence of the string SSS concatenated NNN times. The function will be called multiple times.

Note: Subsequence is any string that can be obtained by deleting zero or more symbols from a given string without changing their order


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, two strings and an integer: S,T,NS, T, NS,T,N.

Note: You need to complete the functions in the submit solution tab:


string patternNash(string S, string T, int n){...}



Output Format
For each testcase, output will be in a single line containing a single string passed by function patternNash and it should be either "YES" or "NO".
constraints: Sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ and ŌłŻTŌłŻ|T|ŌłŻTŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
30 points : Sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ and ŌłŻTŌłŻ|T|ŌłŻTŌłŻ over all test cases does not exceed 100010001000

sampleTestCases.input:
3
aa aaa 2
abc ap 1000
codechef feh 3
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, when we concatenate "aa" and "aa" we get "aaaa". Obviously, "aaa" is subsequence of "aaaa".
In the second test case, the string "abcabcabc....abc" has no character 'p' - so the answer is no.
In the third test case, the string "codechefcodechefcodechef" has a subsequence "feh".

hidden.input:
3
aa aaa 2
abc ap 1000
codechef feh 3
